Arrowhead Stadium renamed 'Kansas City Stadium' for World Cup — transformation underway
articles.summary: GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium is officially renamed Kansas City Stadium for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Capacity expansions, pitch installation, and fan plazas are on schedule.

articles.headline: Argentina vs Algeria confirmed as Kansas City opener — Messi at Arrowhead on June 16
articles.summary: The defending World Cup champions open their Group J campaign at Kansas City Stadium on June 16 at 8pm CT. It will be Messi's first match of what is widely expected to be his final World Cup.

articles.headline: Kansas City lands a World Cup Quarterfinal — July 11 at Arrowhead
articles.summary: Kansas City Stadium will host a FIFA World Cup Quarterfinal on July 11 — placing it among the six elite host venues in the entire tournament. The match could feature any top-8 team on earth.

articles.headline: Dutch invasion: Netherlands fans expected to arrive in force for Group F match in KC
articles.summary: Supporters travel clubs from the Netherlands are already organizing group travel to Kansas City for the June 25 Tunisia vs Netherlands Group F match. Orange is expected to dominate the stadium.

articles.headline: Power & Light District named official FIFA Fan Festival hub for Kansas City
articles.summary: The Power & Light District will serve as Kansas City's official FIFA Fan Festival site with giant screens, live entertainment, international food vendors, and free admission on all match days.

articles.headline: Algeria squad preview: can the Atlas Lions pull a Group J upset on Argentina?
articles.summary: Algeria arrive at the 2026 World Cup with their most balanced squad in years. Their opening match against defending champions Argentina in Kansas City on June 16 is the most anticipated Group J fixture.

articles.headline: Curaçao makes history: island of 160,000 people arrives at their first World Cup
articles.summary: Curaçao, a Dutch Caribbean island nation of roughly 160,000 people, will make their first-ever World Cup appearance at Kansas City Stadium on June 20 vs Ecuador. One of the great underdog stories of 2026.

articles.headline: Egypt vs Iran in Seattle — the most geopolitically charged match of the group stage
articles.summary: June 26 at 8pm PT at Lumen Field. Egypt vs Iran in Group G is being described as one of the most politically significant soccer matches in years. Seattle will be at the center of the World Cup's most complex fixture.

articles.headline: USA vs Australia in Seattle: USMNT brings the World Cup home to Lumen Field
articles.summary: June 19 at noon PT. The US national team plays in Seattle for the first time at a World Cup. The match is expected to be the most-attended and most-energized of Seattle's group stage fixtures.

articles.headline: Belgium squad preview: golden generation's last shot at glory begins in Seattle
articles.summary: Belgium open their 2026 World Cup against Egypt at Lumen Field on June 15. De Bruyne leads a squad many consider aging past its peak — making this tournament the Red Devils' last realistic shot at a first World Cup title.

articles.headline: FIFA confirms all 48 teams certified — World Cup field is set
articles.summary: All intercontinental playoff results are in. The full 48-team lineup for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is officially confirmed.

articles.headline: Seattle transit authority confirms expanded Link Light Rail service for all match days
articles.summary: Sound Transit will run 8-minute service on the 1 Line from 4 hours before each Seattle match until 1 AM. Additional express buses from Bellevue and Redmond.

articles.headline: Ticket market analysis: resale floor stabilizing at 30-day mark
articles.summary: Across most non-host venues, resale floors have stabilized or dropped 10–15% from April peaks. Seattle and LA remain elevated.

articles.headline: Argentina names 26-man squad — Messi confirmed, Álvarez leads attack
articles.summary: Argentina released their preliminary World Cup roster. Lionel Messi is confirmed for what is widely expected to be his final World Cup appearance.

articles.headline: Lumen Field renovation complete — capacity set at 69,000 for World Cup
articles.summary: The $45M FIFA-required upgrades to Lumen Field are complete. Temporary seating adds 8,000 seats. New fan plazas open June 13.

articles.headline: England squad depth at an all-time high heading into 2026
articles.summary: With a golden generation at peak age, England are among the favorites. Manager analysis and tactical preview.

articles.headline: Vancouver sees surge in Seattle-based travel bookings for World Cup matches
articles.summary: BC Place is selling out quickly for Group C matches. Day-trip packages from Seattle starting at $180 all-in are moving fast.

articles.headline: USA squad preview: USMNT's deepest roster in history
articles.summary: The United States opens as co-host favorites to advance from their group. Full squad preview and tactical outlook.

articles.headline: Adidas drops official 2026 World Cup match ball — 'Stella' unveiled
articles.summary: The official 2026 FIFA World Cup match ball is named 'Stella.' Thermally bonded, AI-aerodynamics tested. Available June 1.

articles.headline: Seattle fan guide: the 10 best pre-game spots near Lumen Field
articles.summary: From Pioneer Square dive bars to rooftop views, a practical guide to the best matchday pre-game experiences within walking distance.
